title: kube: Cannot listen on the UDP port: listen udp4 :53: bind: address already in use
body:
### Issue Description

`podman kube play`: cannot listen on the UDP port: listen udp4 :53: bind: address already in use

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use the following k8s manifest
```yaml
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.5.1
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
  labels:
    app: pihole
  name: pihole
spec:
  containers:
  - image: docker.io/pihole/pihole:latest
    name: pihole-1
    env:
    - name: TZ
      value: Asia/Kuala_Lumpur
    - name: DNSMASQ_LISTENING
      value: all
    - name: WEBPASSWORD
      value: password
    ports:
    - containerPort: 53
      hostPort: 53
      protocol: TCP
    - containerPort: 53
      hostPort: 53
      protocol: UDP
    - containerPort: 80
      hostPort: 80
      protocol: TCP
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /etc/pihole:Z
      name: pihole
    - mountPath: /etc/dnsmasq.d:Z
      name: dnsmasq
  volumes:
  - hostPath:
      path: ./etc-pihole
      type: Directory
    name: pihole
  - hostPath:
      path: ./etc-dnsmasq.d
      type: Directory
    name: dnsmasq
```
2. `mkdir etc-{pihole,dnsmasq.d}`
3. `podman kube play pihole.yml`


### Describe the results you received

Pod created but failed to start.
```
root@pihole2:~/podman# podman kube play pihole.yml
[starting container 488f695dde776e1791dd696158a205716e12ba6a3f38fa79106917c927fbc62d: cannot listen on the UDP port: listen udp4 :53: bind: address already in use]
[starting container 488f695dde776e1791dd696158a205716e12ba6a3f38fa79106917c927fbc62d: cannot listen on the UDP port: listen udp4 :53: bind: address already in use starting container c991c5131dca911942f92da7d14d2c7473cfae69db5a4698ae4924e609000ec2: a dependency of container c991c5131dca911942f92da7d14d2c7473cfae69db5a4698ae4924e609000ec2 failed to start: container state improper]
Pod:
4d263a248bd72cec8c2ac60eda223b5db2bb3fe117e09169e793645bc3389ec1
Container:

starting container 488f695dde776e1791dd696158a205716e12ba6a3f38fa79106917c927fbc62d: cannot listen on the UDP port: listen udp4 :53: bind: address already in use
starting container c991c5131dca911942f92da7d14d2c7473cfae69db5a4698ae4924e609000ec2: a dependency of container c991c5131dca911942f92da7d14d2c7473cfae69db5a4698ae4924e609000ec2 failed to start: container state improper

Error: failed to start 2 containers
root@pihole2:~/podman# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                  PORTS
                                                NAMES
488f695dde77  localhost/podman-pause:4.3.1-0                       7 seconds ago  Created                 0.0.0.0:53->53/tcp, 0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:53->53/udp  4d263a248bd7-infra
c991c5131dca  docker.io/pihole/pihole:latest                       4 seconds ago  Created                 0.0.0.0:53->53/tcp, 0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:53->53/udp  pihole-pihole-1
```

### Describe the results you expected

Pod created and started successfully.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Inside LXC container

### Additional information

Run fine if using `podman run`
```sh
sudo podman run -d \
    --name=pihole \
    -e TZ=Asia/Kuala_Lumpur \
    -e DNSMASQ_LISTENING=all \
    -e WEBPASSWORD=password \
    -v ./etc-pihole:/etc/pihole \
    -v ./etc-dnsmasq.d:/etc/dnsmasq.d \
    -p 53:53/tcp \
    -p 53:53/udp \
    -p 80:80/tcp \
    --restart=unless-stopped \
    docker.io/pihole/pihole:latest
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @mangkoran.

> Inside LXC container

Does it work outside the LXC container?
This sounds like you already have a dns server listening on the port, check `ss-ulpn`

> Does it work outside the LXC container?

Not sure as I tested it in Proxmox VE container.

> This sounds like you already have a dns server listening on the port, check ss-ulpn

Debian has no `ss-ulpn` so I used `lsof`.
```
root@pihole2:~# lsof -i -P -n
COMMAND  PID USER   FD   TYPE  DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
systemd    1 root   40u  IPv6 4397161      0t0  TCP *:22 (LISTEN)
sshd     139 root    3u  IPv6 4397161      0t0  TCP *:22 (LISTEN)
master   401 root   13u  IPv4 4398234      0t0  TCP 127.0.0.1:25 (LISTEN)
master   401 root   14u  IPv6 4398235      0t0  TCP [::1]:25 (LISTEN)
sshd    9182 root    4u  IPv6 4917576      0t0  TCP 10.10.1.7:22->192.168.50.128:64591 (ESTABLISHED)
```

The thing is that I could run pihole using `podman run ...` (complete command above), but not using `podman kube play ...`.
```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                  PORTS
                                                NAMES
c6e858a8809c  docker.io/pihole/pihole:latest                       3 hours ago  Up 3 hours ago (healthy)  0.0.0.0:53->53/tcp, 0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:53->53/udp  pihole
```

I also updated the error log in my first post above.
Can you provide the full podman inspect output of the two created containers by the yaml. Also make sure to stop/remove the container created with podman run before you run `kube play`.
Also sorry the command is `ss` so run `ss -ulpn`. You could also use `netstat` if that is installed. But I don't think they would return anything different from `lsof`.

```yaml

    - name: DNSMASQ_LISTENING
      value: all
    ports:

      hostPort: 53

      hostPort: 80
```

I think I understand this now, the reason this is failing is because our dns server aardvark-dns is also trying to bind 53. You need to run either with `--network bridge` which uses a network without our dns server, change the dns_port in containers.conf or set a explicit host ip for you ports, i.e. add `hostIP: <your eth ip>` for you prots

Update: Well after searching the net, this behavior seems coming from [Kubernetes API](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.27/#podspec-v1-core) itself, which has `hostNetwork: false` by default.
